1.arabic: بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ وَجَعَلَ ٱلظُّلُمَـٰتِ وَٱلنُّورَ ۖ ثُمَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِرَبِّهِمْ يَعْدِلُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: ALL PRAISE BE to God who created the heavens and the earth, and ordained darkness and light. Yet the unbelievers make the others equal of their Lord.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: All praise is to Allah, Who has created the heavens and the earth, and has created darkness and light; yet the disbelievers appoint equals (false deities) to their Lord!

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Praise belongs to God who created the heavens and the earth and appointed the shadows and light; then the unbelievers ascribe equals to their Lord.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: All praise unto Allah, who created the heavens and the earth and made the darknesses and the light, yet those who disbelieve equalise others with their Lord.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: All praises and thanks be to Allah, Who (Alone) created the heavens and the earth, and originated the darkness and the light, yet those who disbelieve hold others as equal with their Lord.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Praise be to God, Who created the heavens and the earth, and made the darkness and the light. Yet those who disbelieve ascribe equals to their Lord.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: All praise is for Allah alone, Who created the heavens and the earth, and brought into being light and darkness, and yet those who have rejected the call of the Truth ascribe others to be equals to their Lord.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: All praise be to Allah, Who created the heavens and the earth, and originated the darkness and the light, yet those who disbelieve hold others as equal with their Lord.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Praise be to Allah, Who hath created the heavens and the earth, and hath appointed darkness and light. Yet those who disbelieve ascribe rivals unto their Lord.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: All praise belongs to Allah who created the heavens and the earth and made the darkness and the light. Yet the faithless equate [others] with their Lord.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Praise is due to Allah, who has created the heavens and the earth and made darkness and light. Yet the unbelievers make (others) equal with their Lord.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [All] praise is [due] to Allah, who created the heavens and the earth and made the darkness and the light. Then those who disbelieve equate [others] with their Lord.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Only God Who has created the heavens, the earth, darkness, and light deserves all praise. The disbelievers consider other things equal to Him.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: All praise is due to Allah, Who created the heavens and the earth and made the darkness and the light; yet those who disbelieve set up equals with their Lord.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Praise be to God, who has created the heavens and the earth and brought into being darkness and light. Yet those who deny the truth set up equals to their Lord!

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Praise be Allah, Who created the heavens and the earth, and made the darkness and the light. Yet those who reject Faith hold (others) as equal, with their Guardian-Lord.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Alhamdu lillahi allathee khalaqa alssamawati waalarda wajaAAala alththulumati waalnnoora thumma allatheena kafaroo birabbihim yaAAdiloona

2.arabic: هُوَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَكُم مِّن طِينٍ ثُمَّ قَضَىٰٓ أَجَلًا ۖ وَأَجَلٌ مُّسَمًّى عِندَهُۥ ۖ ثُمَّ أَنتُمْ تَمْتَرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: It is He who created you from clay then determined a term (of life) for you, and a term (is fixed) with Him. Even then you doubt.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: It is He Who has created you from clay, and then decreed a term for you; and it is a fixed promise before Him, yet you still doubt!

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: It is He who created you of clay, then determined a term and a term is stated with Him; yet thereafter you doubt.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: He it is who created you of clay and then decreed a term - and a term determined is with Him - yet ye waver.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: He it is Who has created you from clay, and then has decreed a stated term (for you to die). And there is with Him another determined term (for you to be resurrected), yet you doubt (in the Resurrection).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: It is He who created you from clay, then decided a term—a term determined by him. Yet you doubt.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: He it is Who has created you out of clay, and then decreed a term (of life), and has also appointed another term, a term determined with Him. Yet you are in doubt!

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: He it is Who has created you from clay, and then has decreed a stated term. And there is with Him another determined term, yet you doubt.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He it is Who hath created you from clay, and hath decreed a term for you. A term is fixed with Him. Yet still ye doubt!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: It is He who created you from clay, then ordained the term [of your life]—the specified term is with Him—and yet you are in doubt.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: It is He who has created you from clay. He has decreed a term for you and another one set with Him. Yet you are still in doubt.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: It is He who created you from clay and then decreed a term and a specified time [known] to Him; then [still] you are in dispute.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: It is He who has created you from clay to live for a life-time and the span of your life is only known to Him. You are still suspicious!.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He it is Who created you from clay, then He decreed a term; and there is a term named with Him; still you doubt.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: It is He who has created you out of clay, and then has decreed a term [for you], a term known [only] to Him. Yet you are still in doubt,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: He it is created you from clay, and then decreed a stated term (for you). And there is in His presence another determined term; yet ye doubt within yourselves!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Huwa allathee khalaqakum min teenin thumma qada ajalan waajalun musamman AAindahu thumma antum tamtaroona

3.arabic: وَهُوَ ٱللَّهُ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَفِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ يَعْلَمُ سِرَّكُمْ وَجَهْرَكُمْ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا تَكْسِبُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: He is God in the heavens and the earth. He knows what you hide and bring out into the open, and knows what you earn (of good and evil).

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And He is Allah (the God) of the heavens and in the earth; He knows all, your secrets and your disclosures, and He knows your deeds.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: He is God in the heavens and the earth; He knows your secrets, and what you publish, and He knows what you are earning.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: He is Allah in the heavens and the earth; He knoweth your secret and your publishment, and He knoweth that which ye earn.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And He is Allah (to be worshipped Alone) in the heavens and on the earth, He knows what you conceal and what you reveal, and He knows what you earn (good or bad).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He is God in the heavens and the earth. He knows what you keep secret and what you make public; and He knows what you earn.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And He it is Who is One True God in the heavens and in the earth. He knows your deeds - both secret and open - and knows fully whatever you earn.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And He is Allah, in the heavens and the earth, He knows what you conceal and what you reveal, and He knows what you earn.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He is Allah in the heavens and in the earth. He knoweth both your secret and your utterance, and He knoweth what ye earn.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He is Allah in the heavens and on the earth: He knows your secret and your overt [matters], and He knows what you earn.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: He is Allah in the heavens and earth. He has knowledge of all that you hide and all that you reveal. He knows what you earn.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And He is Allah, [the only deity] in the heavens and the earth. He knows your secret and what you make public, and He knows that which you earn.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: He is God of the heavens and the earth and He knows whatever you conceal, reveal, or gain.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And He is Allah in the heavens and in the earth; He knows your secret (thoughts) and your open (words), and He knows what you earn.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He is God both in the heavens and on earth. He has knowledge of all that you hide and all that you reveal. He knows what you do;

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And He is Allah in the heavens and on earth. He knoweth what ye hide, and what ye reveal, and He knoweth the (recompense) which ye earn (by your deeds).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wahuwa Allahu fee alssamawati wafee alardi yaAAlamu sirrakum wajahrakum wayaAAlamu ma taksiboona

4.arabic: وَمَا تَأْتِيهِم مِّنْ ءَايَةٍ مِّنْ ءَايَـٰتِ رَبِّهِمْ إِلَّا كَانُوا۟ عَنْهَا مُعْرِضِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Yet no sign of their Lord comes to them but they turn away from it.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And never does a sign come to them from among the signs of Allah, except that they* turn away from it. (* The disbelievers)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Not a sign of their Lord comes to them, but they turn away from it.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And not a sign cometh unto them of the signs of their Lord but that therefore they have been backsliders.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And never an Ayah (sign) comes to them from the Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) of their Lord, but that they have been turning away from it.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Not one of their Lord’s signs comes to them, but they turn away from it.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Yet every time a sign of their Lord comes to them, they turn away from it,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And never an Ayah comes to them from the Ayat of their Lord, but that they have been turning away from it.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Never came there unto them a revelation of the revelations of Allah but they did turn away from it.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: There did not come to them any sign from among the signs of their Lord, but that they used to disregard it.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Yet every time a sign comes to them from the signs of their Lord, they turn away from it.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And no sign comes to them from the signs of their Lord except that they turn away therefrom.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (The unbelievers) have always turned away from the revelations and the evidence (of the Truth) that has been sent to them from time to time.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And there does not come to them any communication of the communications of their Lord but they turn aside from it

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: yet every time a sign comes to them from their Lord, they turn away from it.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But never did a single one of the signs of their Lord reach them, but they turned away therefrom.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wama tateehim min ayatin min ayati rabbihim illa kanoo AAanha muAArideena

5.arabic: فَقَدْ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِٱلْحَقِّ لَمَّا جَآءَهُمْ ۖ فَسَوْفَ يَأْتِيهِمْ أَنۢبَـٰٓؤُا۟ مَا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So they disbelieved the truth when it came to them; but they will soon come to know the reality of what they had ridiculed.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So indeed they denied the truth* when it came to them; so now the tidings of the thing they used to mock at, will come to them. (Prophet Mohammed -peace and blessings be upon him, or the Holy Qur’an).

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: They cried lies to the truth when it came to them, but there shall come to them news of that they were mocking.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Surely they have belied the truth when it came unto them; so presently there cometh unto them the tidings of that whereat they have been mocking.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Indeed, they rejected the truth (the Quran and Muhammad SAW) when it came to them, but there will come to them the news of that (the torment) which they used to mock at.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They denied the truth when it has come to them; but soon will reach them the news of what they used to ridicule.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and thus they gave the lie to the Truth that has now come to them. Soon they will come upon some news concerning what they had mocked at.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Indeed, they rejected the truth when it came to them, but there will come to them the news of that which they used to mock at.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And they denied the truth when it came unto them. But there will come unto them the tidings of that which they used to deride.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They have certainly denied the truth when it came to them, but soon there will come to them the news of what they have been deriding.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: They belied the truth when it came to them, but there shall come to them news of that they were mocking.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: For they had denied the truth when it came to them, but there is going to reach them the news of what they used to ridicule.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They have refused the Truth (Muhammad's message) that has come to them but they will soon learn the consequences of their mocking.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So they have indeed rejected the truth when it came to them; therefore the truth of what they mocked at will shine upon them.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They have rejected truth whenever it came to them, yet [more] news will reach them concerning what they have been making a mockery of.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And now they reject the truth when it reaches them: but soon shall they learn the reality of what they used to mock at.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faqad kaththaboo bialhaqqi lamma jaahum fasawfa yateehim anbao ma kanoo bihi yastahzioona

6.arabic: أَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ كَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا مِن قَبْلِهِم مِّن قَرْنٍ مَّكَّنَّـٰهُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مَا لَمْ نُمَكِّن لَّكُمْ وَأَرْسَلْنَا ٱلسَّمَآءَ عَلَيْهِم مِّدْرَارًا وَجَعَلْنَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرَ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهِمْ فَأَهْلَكْنَـٰهُم بِذُنُوبِهِمْ وَأَنشَأْنَا مِنۢ بَعْدِهِمْ قَرْنًا ءَاخَرِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Do they not see how many generations We laid low before them, whom We had firmly established in the land as We have not established you, and showered abundant rain on them, and made rivers lap at their feet, yet whom We destroyed for their sins, and raised new generations after them?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Do they not see how many civilisations We destroyed before them, whom We had established more firmly in the earth than We have established you, and We sent on them abundant rain from the sky, and made the rivers flow beneath them? So we destroyed them because of their sins, and created another civilisation after them.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Have they not regarded how We destroyed before them many a generation We established in the earth, as We never established you, and how We loosed heaven upon them in torrents, and made the rivers to flow beneath them? Then We destroyed them because of their sins, and raised up after them another generation;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Observe they not how many a generation before them We have destroyed whom We had established on the earth as We have not established you, and upon whom We had sent the rains of heaven pouring and under whom We had made the rivers flow; yet We destroyed them for their sins and We produced after them a generation of others.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Have they not seen how many a generation before them We have destroyed whom We had established on the earth such as We have not established you? And We poured out on them rain from the sky in abundance, and made the rivers flow under them. Yet We destroyed them for their sins, and created after them other generations.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Have they not considered how many generations We destroyed before them? We had established them on earth more firmly than We established you, and We sent the clouds pouring down abundant rain on them, and We made rivers flow beneath them. But We destroyed them for their sins, and established other civilizations after them.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Have they not seen how many a people We have destroyed before them? People whom We had made more powerful in the earth than you are and upon them We showered from the heavens abundant rains, and at whose feet We caused the rivers to flow? And then (when they behaved ungratefully) We destroyed them for their sins, and raised other peoples in their place.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Have they not seen how many a generation before them We have destroyed whom We had established on the earth such as We have not established you And We poured out on them rain from the sky in abundance, and made the rivers flow under them. Yet We destroyed them for their sins, and created after them other generations.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: See they not how many a generation We destroyed before them, whom We had established in the earth more firmly than We have established you, and We shed on them abundant showers from the sky, and made the rivers flow beneath them. Yet we destroyed them for their sins, and created after them another generation.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Have they not regarded how many a generation We have destroyed before them whom We had granted power in the land in respects that We did not grant you, and We sent abundant rains for them from the sky and made streams run for them? Then We destroyed them for their sins, and brought forth another generation after them.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Have they not seen how many generations We have destroyed before them whom We had established in the land in a way We never established you, sending down for them abundant water from the sky and made the rivers to flow underneath them? Yet because they sinned, We destroyed them and raised up other generations after them.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Have they not seen how many generations We destroyed before them which We had established upon the earth as We have not established you? And We sent [rain from] the sky upon them in showers and made rivers flow beneath them; then We destroyed them for their sins and brought forth after them a generation of others.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Why do they not consider how many generations living before them We have destroyed. We established those nations in the land with abilities far beyond those given to you. We sent down plenty of rain from the sky for them and made streams flow therein, but, then, We destroyed them for their sins and established other nations after them.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Do they not consider how many a generation We have destroyed before them, whom We had established in the earth as We have not established you, and We sent the clouds pouring rain on them in abundance, and We made the rivers to flow beneath them, then We destroyed them on account of their faults and raised up after them another generation.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Have they not seen how many generations We destroyed before them? We established them on the earth more firmly than you, and We sent the clouds over them, pouring down abundant rain; and made the rivers flow at their feet. Yet We destroyed them for their sins, and raised up other generations after them.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: See they not how many of those before them We did destroy?- generations We had established on the earth, in strength such as We have not given to you - for whom We poured out rain from the skies in abundance, and gave (fertile) streams flowing beneath their (feet): yet for their sins We destroyed them, and raised in their wake fresh generations (to succeed them).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Alam yaraw kam ahlakna min qablihim min qarnin makkannahum fee alardi ma lam numakkin lakum waarsalna alssamaa AAalayhim midraran wajaAAalna alanhara tajree min tahtihim faahlaknahum bithunoobihim waanshana min baAAdihim qarnan akhareena

7.arabic: وَلَوْ نَزَّلْنَا عَلَيْكَ كِتَـٰبًا فِى قِرْطَاسٍ فَلَمَسُوهُ بِأَيْدِيهِمْ لَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ إِنْ هَـٰذَآ إِلَّا سِحْرٌ مُّبِينٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Even if We had sent you a transcript on paper which they could feel with their hands, the unbelievers would have said: "This is nothing but clear sorcery."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And had We sent down to you (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) something written on paper so that they could feel it with their hands, even then the disbelievers would have said, “This is nothing but clear magic.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Had We sent down on thee a Book on parchment and so they touched it with their hands, yet the unbelievers would have said, 'This is naught but manifest sorcery.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And had We sent down unto thee a Book written upon parchment so that they could have touched it with their hands, those who disbelieve would have said: this is naught but magic manifest.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And even if We had sent down unto you (O Muhammad SAW) a Message written on paper so that they could touch it with their hands, the disbelievers would have said: "This is nothing but obvious magic!"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Had We sent down upon you a book on paper, and they had touched it with their hands, those who disbelieve would have said, “This is nothing but plain magic.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (O Messenger!) Had We sent down to you a book incribed on parchment, and had they even touched it with their own hands, the unbelievers would still have said: 'This is nothing but plain magic.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And even if We had sent down unto you a Message written on paper so that they could touch it with their hands, the disbelievers would have said: "This is nothing but obvious magic!"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Had we sent down unto thee (Muhammad) (actual) writing upon parchment, so that they could feel it with their hands, those who disbelieve would have said: This is naught else than mere magic.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Had We sent down to you a Book on paper so they could touch it with their [own] hands, [still] the faithless would have said, ‘This is nothing but plain magic.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Had We sent down to you a Book on parchment and they touched it with their own hands, those who disbelieved would say: 'This is nothing but plain magic'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And even if We had sent down to you, [O Muhammad], a written scripture on a page and they touched it with their hands, the disbelievers would say, "This is not but obvious magic."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad), had We sent you a Book on paper, the unbelievers would have touched it with their hands but would still have said, "It is no more than plain sorcery".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And if We had sent to you a writing on a paper, then they had touched it with their hands, certainly those who disbelieve would have said: This is nothing but clear enchantment.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: But even if We had sent down to you a Book written on parchment, and they had touched it with their own hands -- those who deny the truth would still have said, "This is mere magic."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: If We had sent unto thee a written (message) on parchment, so that they could touch it with their hands, the Unbelievers would have been sure to say: "This is nothing but obvious magic!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walaw nazzalna AAalayka kitaban fee qirtasin falamasoohu biaydeehim laqala allatheena kafaroo in hatha illa sihrun mubeenun

8.arabic: وَقَالُوا۟ لَوْلَآ أُنزِلَ عَلَيْهِ مَلَكٌ ۖ وَلَوْ أَنزَلْنَا مَلَكًا لَّقُضِىَ ٱلْأَمْرُ ثُمَّ لَا يُنظَرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They say: "How is it no angel was sent down to him?" Had We sent an angel down the matter would have come to end, and they would have had no respite.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And they said, “Why has not an angel been sent down to him?” And had We sent down an angel, then the matter would have been finished, and they would not get any respite.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: 'Why has an angel not been sent down on him?' they say; yet had We sent down an angel, the matter would have been determined, and then no respite would be given them.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And they say: wherefore hath not an angel been sent down unto him? Were We to send down an angel, the affair would have been decreed and they would not be respited.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And they say: "Why has not an angel been sent down to him?" Had We sent down an angel, the matter would have been judged at once, and no respite would be granted to them.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And they say, “Why was an angel not sent down to him.” Had We sent down an angel, the matter would have been settled, and they would not have been reprieved.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They also say: 'Why has no angel been sent down to this Prophet?' Had We sent down an angel, the matter would surely have long been decided and no respite would have been granted them.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And they say: "Why has not an angel been sent down to him" Had We sent down an angel, the matter would have been judged at once, and no respite would be granted to them.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They say: Why hath not an angel been sent down unto him? If We sent down an angel, then the matter would be judged; no further time would be allowed them (for reflection).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They say, ‘Why has not an angel been sent down to him?’ Were We to send down an angel, the matter would surely be decided, and then they would not be granted any respite.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: They ask: 'Why has no angel been sent down to him' If We had sent down an angel, their fate would have been determined and they would never have been respited.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And they say, "Why was there not sent down to him an angel?" But if We had sent down an angel, the matter would have been decided; then they would not be reprieved.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They have said, "Why has not an angel come to him (Muhammad)?" Had We sent an angel to them, the matter would have inevitably been out of their hands, and they would have been given no more time.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And they say: Why has not an angel been sent down to him? And had We sent down an angel, the matter would have certainly been decided and then they would not have been respited.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They ask, "Why has an angel not been sent down to him?" If We did send down an angel, the matter would be settled and they would not have been granted any respite.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They say: "Why is not an angel sent down to him?" If we did send down an angel, the matter would be settled at once, and no respite would be granted them.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waqaloo lawla onzila AAalayhi malakun walaw anzalna malakan laqudiya alamru thumma la yuntharoona

9.arabic: وَلَوْ جَعَلْنَـٰهُ مَلَكًا لَّجَعَلْنَـٰهُ رَجُلًا وَلَلَبَسْنَا عَلَيْهِم مَّا يَلْبِسُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Even if We had sent down an angel as messenger he would have appeared in the garb of a man and filled them with confusion, like the one they are filled with.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And had we appointed an angel as a Prophet, We would still have made him as a man and would keep them in the same doubt, as they are now in.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And had We made him an angel, yet assuredly We would have made him a man, and confused for them the thing which they themselves are confusing.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And had We made him an angel, We would still have made him a man, and We would have confounded for them that which they are confounding.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And had We appointed him an angel, We indeed would have made him a man, and We would have certainly caused them confusion in a matter which they have already covered with confusion (i.e. the Message of Prophet Muhammad SAW).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Had We made him an angel, We would have made him a man, and confused them when they are already confused.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Had We appointed an angel, We would have sent him down in the form of a man - and thus We would have caused them the same doubt which they now entertain.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And had We appointed him an angel, We indeed would have made him a man, and We would have certainly caused them confusion in a matter which they have already covered with confusion.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Had we appointed him (Our messenger) an angel, We assuredly had made him (as) a man (that he might speak to men); and (thus) obscured for them (the truth) they (now) obscure.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Had We made him an angel, We would have surely made him a man, and We would have surely confounded them just as they confound [the truth now].

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: If We had made him an angel, We would have given him the resemblance of a man, and would have as such confused them with that in which they are already confused.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And if We had made him an angel, We would have made him [appear as] a man, and We would have covered them with that in which they cover themselves.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Were We to have made him (Our Messenger) an angel, We would have made him resemble a human being and they would have again complained that the matter was as confusing to them as it is to them now.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And if We had made him angel, We would certainly have made him a man, and We would certainly have made confused to them what they make confused.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Indeed, if We had sent an angel as messenger, We would have made him in the form of a man as well, and would have thus added to their confusion.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: If We had made it an angel, We should have sent him as a man, and We should certainly have caused them confusion in a matter which they have already covered with confusion.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walaw jaAAalnahu malakan lajaAAalnahu rajulan walalabasna AAalayhim ma yalbisoona

10.arabic: وَلَقَدِ ٱسْتُهْزِئَ بِرُسُلٍ مِّن قَبْلِكَ فَحَاقَ بِٱلَّذِينَ سَخِرُوا۟ مِنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Surely the apostles have been mocked before you; but what they had mocked rebounded on the mockers themselves.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And certainly, O dear Prophet (Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) the Noble Messengers before you have also been mocked at, so those who laughed at them were themselves ruined by their own mocking.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Messengers indeed were mocked at before thee; but those that scoffed at them were encompassed by that they mocked at.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And assuredly mocked were the apostles before thee, wherefore that whereat they scoffed beset these who had been mocking.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And indeed (many) Messengers were mocked before you, but their scoffers were surrounded by the very thing that they used to mock at.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Messengers before you were ridiculed, but those who mocked them became besieged by what they ridiculed.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And indeed before your time (O Muhammad!) many a Messenger has been scoffed at; but those who mocked at them were encompassed by the Truth they had scoffed at.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And indeed Messengers were mocked before you, but their scoffers were surrounded by the very thing that they used to mock at.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Messengers (of Allah) have been derided before thee, but that whereat they scoffed surrounded such of them as did deride.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Apostles were certainly derided before you. Then those who ridiculed them were besieged by what they used to deride.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Other Messengers have been mocked before you. But those who scoffed at them were encompassed by that they had mocked'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And already were messengers ridiculed before you, but those who mocked them were enveloped by that which they used to ridicule.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They mocked the Messengers who lived before you (Muhammad), but those who received warnings of punishment and mocked the warnings, all suffered their torments accordingly.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And certainly apostles before you were mocked at, but that which they mocked at encompassed the scoffers among them.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Messengers have been mocked before you, but those who scoffed were overtaken by the very thing they scoffed at.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Mocked were (many) messengers before thee; but their scoffers were hemmed in by the thing that they mocked.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walaqadi istuhzia birusulin min qablika fahaqa biallatheena sakhiroo minhum ma kanoo bihi yastahzioona

11.arabic: قُلْ سِيرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ثُمَّ ٱنظُرُوا۟ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلْمُكَذِّبِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Say: "Travel in the land and see what happened to those who disbelieved."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Proclaim, “Travel in the land, and see what sort of fate befell those who denied.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Say: 'Journey in the land, then behold how was the end of them that cried lies.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Say thou: go about in the earth and then see what wise hath been the end of the beliers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "Travel in the land and see what was the end of those who rejected truth."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Say, “Travel the earth and observe the final fate of the deniers.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Say: 'Go about journeying the earth, and behold the end of those who gave the lie (to the Truth).'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Say: "Travel in the land and see what was the end of those who rejected truth."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Say (unto the disbelievers): Travel in the land, and see the nature of the consequence for the rejecters!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Say, ‘Travel over the land, and then observe how was the fate of the deniers.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Say: 'Travel the land and see what was the fate of those who belied'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Say, "Travel through the land; then observe how was the end of the deniers."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad), tell them to travel in the land and see what happened to those who rejected the Truth.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Say: Travel in the land, then see what was the end of the rejecters.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Say, "Travel about the land and see what was the end of the deniers."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Say: "Travel through the earth and see what was the end of those who rejected Truth."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qul seeroo fee alardi thumma onthuroo kayfa kana AAaqibatu almukaththibeena

12.arabic: قُل لِّمَن مَّا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ قُل لِّلَّهِ ۚ كَتَبَ عَلَىٰ نَفْسِهِ ٱلرَّحْمَةَ ۚ لَيَجْمَعَنَّكُمْ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهِ ۚ ٱلَّذِينَ خَسِرُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ فَهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And ask: "To whom belongs what is in the heavens and the earth?" Say: "To God." He has prescribed grace for Himself. He will gather you on the Day of Resurrection which is certain to come. Only they who are lost of soul will not come to believe.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Say, “To Whom does all whatever is in the heavens and the earth, belong?” Proclaim, “To Allah”; He has made mercy obligatory upon His grace; undoubtedly, He will surely gather you all together on the Day of Resurrection in which there is no doubt; those who put their souls to ruin, do not accept faith.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Say: 'To whom belongs what is in the heavens and in the earth?' Say: 'It is God's. He has prescribed for Himself mercy. He will surely gather you to the Resurrection Day, of which is no doubt. Those who have lost their souls, they do not believe.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Say thou: whose is whatsoever is in the heavens and the earth? Say thou: Allah's. He hath prescribed mercy for Himself. Surely He shall gather you together on the Day of Judgement whereof there is no doubt. Those who have lost themselves shall not believe.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "To whom belongs all that is in the heavens and the earth?" Say: "To Allah. He has prescribed Mercy for Himself. Indeed He will gather you together on the Day of Resurrection, about which there is no doubt. Those who destroy themselves will not believe [in Allah as being the only Ilah (God), and Muhammad SAW as being one of His Messengers, and in Resurrection, etc.].

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Say, “To whom belongs what is in the heavens and the earth?” Say, “To God.” He has inscribed for Himself mercy. He will gather you to the Day of Resurrection, in which there is no doubt. Those who lost their souls do not believe.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Ask thern: 'To whom belongs all that is in the heavens and on the earth?' Say: 'Everything belongs to Allah.' He has bound Himself to the exercise of mercy (and thus does not chastise you for your disobedience and excesses instantly). Surely He will gather you all together on the Day of Resurrection - the coming of which is beyond doubt; but those who have courted their own ruin are not going to believe.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Say: "To whom belongs all that is in the heavens and the earth" Say: "To Allah." He has prescribed mercy for Himself. Indeed He will gather you together on the Day of Resurrection, about which there is no doubt. Those who destroy themselves will not believe.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Say: Unto whom belongeth whatsoever is in the heavens and the earth? Say: Unto Allah. He hath prescribed for Himself mercy, that He may bring you all together to the Day of Resurrection whereof there is no doubt. Those who ruin their souls will not believe.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Say, ‘To whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth?’ Say, ‘To Allah. He has made mercy incumbent upon Himself. He will surely gather you on the Day of Resurrection, in which there is no doubt. Those who have ruined their souls will not have faith.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Say: 'To whom belongs that which is in the heavens and the earth' Say: 'To Allah. He has written for Himself mercy, and will gather you on the Day ofResurrection in which there is no doubt. Those who have lost their souls, they do not believe'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Say, "To whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and earth?" Say, "To Allah." He has decreed upon Himself mercy. He will surely assemble you for the Day of Resurrection, about which there is no doubt. Those who will lose themselves [that Day] do not believe.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Ask them, "Who is the owner of the heavens and the earth besides God, the All-merciful, who will gather you all together on the Day of Judgment? That day will certainly come. Those who have lost their souls will not believe.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Say: To whom belongs what is in the heavens and the earth? Say: To Allah; He has ordained mercy on Himself; most certainly He will gather you on the resurrection day-- there is no doubt about it. (As for) those who have lost their souls, they will not believe.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Say, "To whom belongs all that is in the heavens and earth?" Say, "To God. He has taken it upon Himself to be merciful. That He will gather you on the Day of Resurrection is beyond all doubt. Those who have forfeited their souls will never have faith."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Say: "To whom belongeth all that is in the heavens and on earth?" Say: "To Allah. He hath inscribed for Himself (the rule of) Mercy. That He will gather you together for the Day of Judgment, there is no doubt whatever. It is they who have lost their own souls, that will not believe.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qul liman ma fee alssamawati waalardi qul lillahi kataba AAala nafsihi alrrahmata layajmaAAannakum ila yawmi alqiyamati la rayba feehi allatheena khasiroo anfusahum fahum la yuminoona

13.arabic: وَلَهُۥ مَا سَكَنَ فِى ٱلَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Whatsoever dwells in the night and day belongs to Him. He is all-hearing and all-knowing.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And to Him only belongs all whatever exists in the night and in the day; and He only is the All Hearing, the All Knowing.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And to Him belongs whatsoever inhabits the night and the day; and He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: His is whatsoever dwelleth in the night and the day; and He is the Hearer, the Knower.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And to Him belongs whatsoever exists in the night and the day, and He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: To Him belongs whatever rests in the night and the day. He is the Hearing, the Knowing.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And to Him belongs all that dwells in the night and the day. He is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And to Him belongs whatsoever exists in the night and the day, and He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Unto Him belongeth whatsoever resteth in the night and the day. He is the Hearer, the Knower.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: To Him belongs whatever abides in the night and the day, and He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: His is whatever is at rest in the night and in the day. He is the Hearing, the Knowing.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And to Him belongs that which reposes by night and by day, and He is the Hearing, the Knowing.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: To Him belongs all that rests during the night and the day. He is All-hearing and All-knowing."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And to Him belongs whatever dwells in the night and the day; and He is the Hearing, the Knowing.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: To Him belongs all that dwells in the night and the day. He is the All Hearing and the All Knowing.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: To him belongeth all that dwelleth (or lurketh) in the night and the day. For he is the one who heareth and knoweth all things."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walahu ma sakana fee allayli waalnnahari wahuwa alssameeAAu alAAaleemu

14.arabic: قُلْ أَغَيْرَ ٱللَّهِ أَتَّخِذُ وَلِيًّا فَاطِرِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَهُوَ يُطْعِمُ وَلَا يُطْعَمُ ۗ قُلْ إِنِّىٓ أُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ أَوَّلَ مَنْ أَسْلَمَ ۖ وَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Say: "Should I find some other protector besides God the Creator of the heavens and the earth, who nourishes all and is nourished by none? Say: "I am commanded to be the first to submit to Him, and not be an idolater."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Say, “Shall I choose as a supporter someone other than Allah, Who is the Originator of the heavens and the earth and Who feeds and does not need to eat?” Say, “I have been ordered to be the first to submit myself (to Him), and O people, do not be of the polytheists.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Say: 'Shall I take to myself as protector other than God, the Originator of the heavens and of the earth, He. who feeds and is not fed?' Say: 'I have been commanded to be the first of them that surrender: "Be not thou of the idolaters."'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Say thou: Shall I take for a patron any other than Allah, the Maker of the heavens and the earth! And He feedeth, and is not fed. Say thou: I am commanded that I be the first who submitteth himself, and that: be thou not of the associaters.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "Shall I take as a Wali (helper, protector, etc.) any other than Allah, the Creator of the heavens and the earth? And it is He Who feeds but is not fed." Say: "Verily, I am commanded to be the first of those who submit themselves to Allah (as Muslims)." And be not you (O Muhammad SAW) of the Mushrikun [polytheists, pagans, idolaters and disbelievers in the Oneness of Allah].

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Say, “Shall I take for myself a protector other than God, Originator of the heavens and the earth, and He feeds and is not fed?” Say, “I am instructed to be the first of those who submit.” And do not be among the idolaters.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Say: 'Shall I take for my guardian anyone other than Allah - the Originator of the heavens and earth; He Who feeds and Himself is not fed?' Say: 'Surely I have been commanded to be the first among those who submit (to Allah) and not to be one of those who associate others with Allah in His divinity (even though others may do so).'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Say: "Shall I take as a guardian any other than Allah, the Creator of the heavens and the earth And it is He Who feeds but is not fed." Say: "Verily, I am commanded to be the first of those who submit. "And be not you of the idolators.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Say: Shall I choose for a protecting friend other than Allah, the Originator of the heavens and the earth, Who feedeth and is never fed? Say: I am ordered to be the first to surrender (unto Him). And be not thou (O Muhammad) of the idolaters.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Say, ‘Shall I take for guardian [anyone] other than Allah, the originator of the heavens and the earth, who feeds and is not fed?’ Say, ‘I have been commanded to be the first of those who submit [to Allah], and [told,] “Never be one of the polytheists.” ’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Say: 'Should I take any but Allah for a guardian? He is the Originator of the heavens and the earth. He feeds and is not fed' Say: 'I was commanded to be the first to submit to Him' Do not be one of the idolaters.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Say, "Is it other than Allah I should take as a protector, Creator of the heavens and the earth, while it is He who feeds and is not fed?" Say, [O Muhammad], "Indeed, I have been commanded to be the first [among you] who submit [to Allah] and [was commanded], 'Do not ever be of the polytheists.' "

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad), ask them, "Should I take a guardian other than God, the Originator of the heavens and the earth, who feeds everyone and who needs no food Himself?" Say, "I have been commanded to be the first Muslim (submitted to the will of God). Thus, people, do not be pagans."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Say: Shall I take a guardian besides Allah, the Originator of the heavens and the earth, and He feeds (others) and is not (Himself) fed. Say: I am commanded to be the first who submits himself, and you should not be of the polytheists.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Say, "Shall I take as my protector someone other than God, Creator of the heavens and the earth, who feeds all and is fed by none?" Say, "I have been commanded to be the first of those who submit. Do not be one of the polytheists."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Say: "Shall I take for my protector any other than Allah, the Maker of the heavens and the earth? And He it is that feedeth but is not fed." Say: "Nay! but I am commanded to be the first of those who bow to Allah (in Islam), and be not thou of the company of those who join gods with Allah."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qul aghayra Allahi attakhithu waliyyan fatiri alssamawati waalardi wahuwa yutAAimu wala yutAAamu qul innee omirtu an akoona awwala man aslama wala takoonanna mina almushrikeena

15.arabic: قُلْ إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ إِنْ عَصَيْتُ رَبِّى عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Say: "If I disobey my Lord, I fear the punishment of a grievous Day."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Say, “If I disobey my Lord, I then fear the punishment of the Great Day (of Resurrection).”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Say: 'Indeed I fear, if I should rebel against thy Lord, the chastisement of a dreadful day.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Say thou: verily fear, if disobey my Lord, the torment of a Mighty Day.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Say: "I fear, if I disobey my Lord, the torment of a Mighty Day."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Say, “I fear, should I defy my Lord, the punishment of a tremendous Day.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Say: 'Surely do I fear, if I disobey my Lord, the chastisement of an awesome Day.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Say: "I fear, if I disobey my Lord, the torment of a Mighty Day."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Say: I fear, if I rebel against my Lord, the retribution of an Awful Day.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Say, ‘Indeed, should I disobey my Lord, I fear the punishment of a tremendous day.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Say: 'Indeed I fear, if I should disobey my Lord, the punishment of a great Day'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Say, "Indeed I fear, if I should disobey my Lord, the punishment of a tremendous Day."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad), say, "Because of the torment of the Great Day, I am afraid to disobey my Lord".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Say: Surely I fear, if I disobey my Lord, the chastisement of a grievous day.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Say, "I will never disobey my Lord, for I fear the punishment of a dreadful Day."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Say: "I would, if I disobeyed my Lord, indeed have fear of the penalty of a Mighty Day.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qul innee akhafu in AAasaytu rabbee AAathaba yawmin AAatheemin

16.arabic: مَّن يُصْرَفْ عَنْهُ يَوْمَئِذٍ فَقَدْ رَحِمَهُۥ ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْمُبِينُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Whosoever is spared that Day will surely have mercy shown to him, and this will be a clear triumph.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Indeed Allah’s mercy has been upon him, from whom the punishment has been averted on that Day; and this is the clear success.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: From whomsoever it is averted on that day, He will have mercy on him; that is the manifest triumph.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: From whomsoever it is averted on that Day, upon him indeed He has had mercy; that is a supreme achievement.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Who is averted from (such a torment) on that Day, (Allah) has surely been Merciful to him. And that would be the obvious success.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Whoever is spared on that Day—He had mercy on him. That is the clear victory.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Whosoever has been spared chastisement on that Day, Allah has bestowed His mercy upon him. That is the manifest triumph.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Who is averted from (such a torment) on that Day; then He (Allah) has surely been Merciful to him. And that would be the obvious success.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He from whom (such retribution) is averted on that day, (Allah) hath in truth had mercy on him. That will be the signal triumph.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Whoever is spared of it on that day, He has certainly been merciful to him, and that is manifest success.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: From whomsoever it is averted on that Day, He will have mercy on him; that is a clear triumph.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: He from whom it is averted that Day - [Allah] has granted him mercy. And that is the clear attainment.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: One who will be saved from the torment on the Day of Judgment will certainly have received God's mercy and this will be a manifest triumph.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He from whom it is averted on that day, Allah indeed has shown mercy to him; and this is a manifest achievement.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Anyone from whom punishment is averted on that Day has been shown great mercy by God. That is a supreme achievement.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "On that day, if the penalty is averted from any, it is due to Allah's mercy; And that would be (Salvation), the obvious fulfilment of all desire.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Man yusraf AAanhu yawmaithin faqad rahimahu wathalika alfawzu almubeenu

17.arabic: وَإِن يَمْسَسْكَ ٱللَّهُ بِضُرٍّ فَلَا كَاشِفَ لَهُۥٓ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ وَإِن يَمْسَسْكَ بِخَيْرٍ فَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: If God sends you harm, there is no one but He who can take it away; and if He bring you good, surely He has power over everything.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And if Allah afflicts you with some misfortune, then there is none who can remove it, except Him; and if He sends you some good fortune, then (know that) He is Able to do all things.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And if God visits thee with affliction none can remove it but He; and if He visits thee with good, He is powerful over everything.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: If Allah touch thee with hurt there is no reverser thereof but he, and if he touch thee with good, then He is over everything Potent.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And if Allah touches you with harm, none can remove it but He, and if He touches you with good, then He is Able to do all things.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: If God touches you with adversity, none can remove it except He. And if He touches you with good—He is Capable of everything.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Should Allah touch you with affliction, there is none to remove it but He; and should He touch you with good, He has the power to do everything.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And if Allah touches you with harm, none can remove it but He, and if He touches you with good, then He is able to do all things.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: If Allah touch thee with affliction, there is none that can relieve therefrom save Him, and if He touch thee with good fortune (there is none that can impair it); for He is Able to do all things.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Should Allah visit you with some distress there is no one to remove it except Him; and should He bring you some good, then He has power over all things.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: If Allah visits you with affliction, none can remove it except He; and if He touches you with good, indeed, He has power over all things.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And if Allah should touch you with adversity, there is no remover of it except Him. And if He touches you with good - then He is over all things competent.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad), if God afflicts you with hardship, no one besides Him will be able to relieve you. If He bestows a favor on you, know that He has power over all things.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And if Allah touch you with affliction, there is none to take it off but He; and if He visit you with good, then He has power over all things.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: If God should let any harm touch you, no one could remove it except He; while if He should let some good touch you, know that He has the power to do all that He wills.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "If Allah touch thee with affliction, none can remove it but He; if He touch thee with happiness, He hath power over all things.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wain yamsaska Allahu bidurrin fala kashifa lahu illa huwa wain yamsaska bikhayrin fahuwa AAala kulli shayin qadeerun

18.arabic: وَهُوَ ٱلْقَاهِرُ فَوْقَ عِبَادِهِۦ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلْحَكِيمُ ٱلْخَبِيرُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: It is He who prevails over His creatures, and He is all-wise and aware.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: He is the Omnipotent over His bondmen; and He is the Wise, the Aware.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: He is Omnipotent over His servants, and He is the All-wise, the All-aware. Say: 'What thing is greatest in testimony?'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: He is the Supreme above His creatures; and He is the Wise, the Aware.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And He is the Irresistible, above His slaves, and He is the All-Wise, Well-Acquainted with all things.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He is the Supreme over His servants. He is the Wise, the Expert.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: He has the supreme hold over His servants. He is All-Wise, All-Aware.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And He is the Irresistible, above His servants, and He is the All-Wise, Well-Acquainted with all things.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He is the Omnipotent over His slaves, and He is the Wise, the Knower.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And He is the All-dominant over His servants, and He is the All-wise, the All-aware.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: He is the Conqueror over His worshipers. He is the Wise, the Aware.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And He is the subjugator over His servants. And He is the Wise, the Acquainted [with all].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: He is Wise, Well Aware and Dominant over all His creatures.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And He is the Supreme, above His servants; and He is the Wise, the Aware.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He reigns Supreme over His servants; and He is the All Wise, the All Aware.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "He is the irresistible, (watching) from above over His worshippers; and He is the Wise, acquainted with all things."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wahuwa alqahiru fawqa AAibadihi wahuwa alhakeemu alkhabeeru

19.arabic: قُلْ أَىُّ شَىْءٍ أَكْبَرُ شَهَـٰدَةً ۖ قُلِ ٱللَّهُ ۖ شَهِيدٌۢ بَيْنِى وَبَيْنَكُمْ ۚ وَأُوحِىَ إِلَىَّ هَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانُ لِأُنذِرَكُم بِهِۦ وَمَنۢ بَلَغَ ۚ أَئِنَّكُمْ لَتَشْهَدُونَ أَنَّ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ ءَالِهَةً أُخْرَىٰ ۚ قُل لَّآ أَشْهَدُ ۚ قُلْ إِنَّمَا هُوَ إِلَـٰهٌ وَٰحِدٌ وَإِنَّنِى بَرِىٓءٌ مِّمَّا تُشْرِكُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Ask: "Of all things what is most vital as evidence?" Say: "God (who) is witness between you and me that this Qur'an has been revealed to me that I may warn you on its strength, and those whom it reaches. Do you really bear witness there are other gods with God?" Tell them: "I bear no such Witness." Say: "Verily He is the only God, and I am clear of what you associate (with Him)."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Proclaim (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “Whose testimony is the greatest?” Say, “Allah is the Witness between me and you; and this Qur’an has been sent down upon me, so I may warn you with it and whomever it may reach; so do you bear witness that there are other Gods along with Allah?” Say, “I do not bear witness to it”; Say, “He is the only One God and I do not have any relation with whatever you ascribe as partners (to Him).”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Say: 'God is witness between me and you, and this Koran has been revealed to me that I may warn you thereby, and whomsoever it may reach. Do you indeed testify that there are other gods with God?' Say: 'I do not testify.' Say: 'He is only One God, and I am quit of that you associate.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Say thou: what thing is the greatest in testimony? Say thou: Allah is Witness between me and you, and this Qur'an hath been revealed unto me that I may thereby warn you and whomsoever it may reach. Would ye indeed testify that there is anot her god together with Allah? Say thou: I testify not. Say thou: verily He is the One God, and I am quit of that which ye associate.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "What thing is the most great in witness?" Say: "Allah (the Most Great!) is Witness between me and you; this Quran has been revealed to me that I may therewith warn you and whomsoever it may reach. Can you verily bear witness that besides Allah there are other aliha (gods)?" Say "I bear no (such) witness!" Say: "But in truth He (Allah) is the only one Ilah (God). And truly I am innocent of what you join in worship with Him."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Say, “What thing is more solemn in testimony?” Say, “God is Witness between you and me. This Quran was revealed to me, that I may warn you with it, and whomever it may reach. Do you indeed testify that there are other gods with God?” Say, “I myself do not testify.” Say, “He is but One God, and I am innocent of your idolatry.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Ask them: 'Whose testimony is the greatest?' Say: 'Allah is the witness between me and you; and this Qur'an was revealed to me that I should warn you thereby and also whomsoever it may reach.' Do you indeed testify that there are other gods with Allah? Say: 'I shall never testify such a thing.' Say: 'He is the One God and I am altogether averse to all that you associate with Him in His divinity.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Say: "What thing is the most great in witness" Say: "Allah is Witness between you and I; this Qur'an has been revealed to me that I may therewith warn you and whomsoever it may reach. Can you verily bear witness that besides Allah there are other gods" Say "I bear no (such) witness!" Say: "Only He is God, alone, and truly I am innocent of what you join in worship with Him."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Say (O Muhammad): What thing is of most weight in testimony? Say: Allah is Witness between me and you. And this Qur'an hath been inspired in me, that I may warn therewith you and whomsoever it may reach. Do ye in sooth bear witness that there are gods beside Allah? Say: I bear no such witness. Say: He is only One Allah. Lo! I am innocent of that which ye associate (with Him).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Say, ‘What thing is greatest as witness?’ Say, ‘Allah! [He is] witness between me and you, and this Quran has been revealed to me that I may warn thereby you and whomever it may reach.’ ‘Do you indeed bear witness that there are other gods besides Allah?’ Say, ‘I do not bear witness [to any such thing].’ Say, ‘Indeed He is the One God, and I indeed disown what you associate [with Him].’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Say: 'What thing is greatest in testimony' Say: 'Allah is a witness between me and you. This Koran has been revealed to me in order that I can warn you and all whom it reaches. Do you indeed testify that there are gods other than Allah' Say: 'I do not testify' Say: 'He is only One God, and I am quit of that which you associate'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Say, "What thing is greatest in testimony?" Say, "Allah is witness between me and you. And this Qur'an was revealed to me that I may warn you thereby and whomever it reaches. Do you [truly] testify that with Allah there are other deities?" Say, "I will not testify [with you]." Say, "Indeed, He is but one God, and indeed, I am free of what you associate [with Him]."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad), ask them, "What is the greatest testimony? God testifies of my truthfulness to you. He has revealed this Quran to me to warn you and the coming generations (against disobeying God). Do you believe that other gods exist besides God? I solemnly declare that He is the only Lord and that I am not guilty of believing in what the pagans believe."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Say: What thing is the weightiest in testimony? Say: Allah is witness between you and me; and this Quran has been revealed to me that with it I may warn you and whomsoever it reaches. Do you really bear witness that there are other gods with Allah? Say: I do not bear witness. Say: He is only one Allah, and surely I am clear of that which you set up (with Him).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Ask them, "What carries the most weight as a witness?" Tell them it is God. He is a witness between you and me. Say, "This Quran has been revealed to me so that through it I may warn you and whoever it reaches. Do you really bear witness that there are other deities beside God?" Say, "I do not bear witness to this." Say, "He is only one God, and I disown whatever you associate with Him."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Say: "What thing is most weighty in evidence?" Say: "Allah is witness between me and you; This Qur'an hath been revealed to me by inspiration, that I may warn you and all whom it reaches. Can ye possibly bear witness that besides Allah there is another Allah?" Say: "Nay! I cannot bear witness!" Say: "But in truth He is the one Allah, and I truly am innocent of (your blasphemy of) joining others with Him."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qul ayyu shayin akbaru shahadatan quli Allahu shaheedun baynee wabaynakum waoohiya ilayya hatha alquranu lionthirakum bihi waman balagha ainnakum latashhadoona anna maAAa Allahi alihatan okhra qul la ashhadu qul innama huwa ilahun wahidun wainnanee bareeon mimma tushrikoona

20.arabic: ٱلَّذِينَ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُمُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ يَعْرِفُونَهُۥ كَمَا يَعْرِفُونَ أَبْنَآءَهُمُ ۘ ٱلَّذِينَ خَسِرُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ فَهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Those to whom We have given the Book know it distinctly as they know their sons; but those who are lost of soul do not believe.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: The people to whom We gave the Book(s) recognise this Prophet (Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) as they recognise their own sons; those who put their own souls to ruin, do not accept faith.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Those to whom We have given the Book recognize it as they recognize their sons. Those who have lost their own souls, they do not believe.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Those whom We have vouchsafed the Book recognise him even as they recognise their own children. Yet those who have lost themselves will not believe.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Those to whom We have given the Scripture (Jews and Christians) recognize him (i.e. Muhammad SAW as a Messenger of Allah, and they also know that there is no Ilah (God) but Allah and Islam is Allah's Religion), as they recognize their own sons. Those who destroy themselves will not believe. (Tafsir At-Tabari)

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those to whom We have given the Book recognize it as they recognize their own children; but those who have lost their souls do not believe.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Those whom We have given the Book will recognize this just as they recognize their own ofspring; but those who have courted their own ruin will not believe.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Those to whom We have given the Scripture recognize him, as they recognize their own children. Those who have lost (destroyed) themselves will not believe.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Those unto whom We gave the Scripture recognise (this revelation) as they recognise their sons. Those who ruin their own souls will not believe.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Those whom We have given the Book recognize him just as they recognize their sons. Those who have ruined their souls will not have faith.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Those to whom We have given the Book know him (Prophet Muhammad) as they know their own children. But those who have lost their own souls do not believe.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Those to whom We have given the Scripture recognize it as they recognize their [own] sons. Those who will lose themselves [in the Hereafter] do not believe.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The People of the Book know him (Muhammad) just as well as they know their own children, but those who have lost their souls will not believe.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Those whom We have given the Book recognize him as they recognize their sons; (as for) those who have lost their souls, they will not believe.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Those to whom We have given the Scriptures know this as they know their own sons. But those who have ruined their souls will not believe.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Those to whom We have given the Book know this as they know their own sons. Those who have lost their own souls refuse therefore to believe.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Allatheena ataynahumu alkitaba yaAArifoonahu kama yaAArifoona abnaahum allatheena khasiroo anfusahum fahum la yuminoona

21.arabic: وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ ٱفْتَرَىٰ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبًا أَوْ كَذَّبَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِهِۦٓ ۗ إِنَّهُۥ لَا يُفْلِحُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And who is more wicked than he who invents lies about God or denies His revelations? Surely the wicked will not succeed.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And who is more unjust than one who fabricates lies against Allah or denies His signs? Undoubtedly, the unjust will never succeed.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And who does greater evil than he who forges against God a lie, or cries lies to His signs? They shall not prosper, the evildoers.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And who is a greater wrong-doer than he who fabricateth a lie against Allah or belieth His signs? Verily the wrong-doers shall not fare well.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And who does more wrong than he who invents a lie against Allah or rejects His Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, revelations, etc.)? Verily, the Zalimun (polytheists and wrong-doers, etc.) shall never be successful.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Who does greater wrong than someone who fabricates lies against God, or denies His revelations? The wrongdoers will not succeed.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And who could be more wrong-doing than he who either foists a lie on Allah or gives the lie to His signs? Surely such wrong-doers shall not attain success.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And who does more wrong than he who invents a lie against Allah or rejects His Ayat Verily, the wrongdoers shall never be successful.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Who doth greater wrong than he who inventeth a lie against Allah or denieth His revelations? Lo! the wrongdoers will not be successful.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Who is a greater wrongdoer than him who fabricates a lie against Allah, or denies His signs? Indeed the wrongdoers will not be felicitous.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Who is more wicked than he who invents a lie about Allah or belies His verses? The harmdoers shall never prosper.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And who is more unjust than one who invents about Allah a lie or denies His verses? Indeed, the wrongdoers will not succeed.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Who are more unjust than those who ascribe falsehood to God or reject His revelations? The unjust will certainly have no happiness.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And who is more unjust than he who forges a lie against Allah or (he who) gives the lie to His communications; surely the unjust will not be successful.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Who does greater wrong than he who invents a lie against God and denies His signs? Assuredly, the wrongdoers will not succeed.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Who doth more wrong than he who inventeth a lie against Allah or rejecteth His signs? But verily the wrong-doers never shall prosper.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waman athlamu mimmani iftara AAala Allahi kathiban aw kaththaba biayatihi innahu la yuflihu alththalimoona

22.arabic: وَيَوْمَ نَحْشُرُهُمْ جَمِيعًا ثُمَّ نَقُولُ لِلَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوٓا۟ أَيْنَ شُرَكَآؤُكُمُ ٱلَّذِينَ كُنتُمْ تَزْعُمُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The day We shall gather all of them together and say to those who ascribe (partners to God): "Where are the compeers who you claimed (were equal to God)?"

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
