translations.text: Say: Lo! As for me, my Lord hath guided me unto a straight path, a right religion, the community of Abraham, the upright, who was no idolater.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Say, ‘Indeed my Lord has guided me to a straight path, the upright religion, the creed of Abraham, a Hanif, and he was not one of the polytheists.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Say: 'My Lord has guided me to a Straight Path, an upright religion, the creed of Abraham, he was upright, not from the idolaters'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Say, "Indeed, my Lord has guided me to a straight path - a correct religion - the way of Abraham, inclining toward truth. And he was not among those who associated others with Allah."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad), tell them, "My Lord has guided me to the right path, a well established religion and the upright tradition of Abraham who was not a pagan".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Say: Surely, (as for) me, my Lord has guided me to the right path; (to) a most right religion, the faith of Ibrahim the upright one, and he was not of the polytheists.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Say, "My Lord has guided me to a straight path, and to an upright religion, the religion of Abraham the upright, who was not of those who associate partners with God."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Say: "Verily, my Lord hath guided me to a way that is straight,- a religion of right,- the path (trod) by Abraham the true in Faith, and he (certainly) joined not gods with Allah."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qul innanee hadanee rabbee ila siratin mustaqeemin deenan qiyaman millata ibraheema haneefan wama kana mina almushrikeena

162.arabic: قُلْ إِنَّ صَلَاتِى وَنُسُكِى وَمَحْيَاىَ وَمَمَاتِى لِلَّهِ رَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Tell them: "My service and sacrifice, my life and my death, are all of them for God, the creator and Lord of all the worlds.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Say, “Undoubtedly my prayers and my sacrifices, and my living and my dying are all for Allah, the Lord Of The Creation.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Say: 'My prayer, my ritual sacrifice, my living, my dying -- all belongs to God, the Lord of all Being.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Say thou: verily my prayer and my rites and my living and my dying are all for Allah, Lord of the worlds.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "Verily, my Salat (prayer), my sacrifice, my living, and my dying are for Allah, the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Say, “My prayer and my worship, and my life and my death, are devoted to God, the Lord of the Worlds.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Say: 'Surely my Prayer, all my acts of worship, and my living and my dying are for Allah alone, the Lord of the whole universe.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Say: "Verily, my Salah, my sacrifice, my living, and my dying are for Allah, the Lord of all that exists."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Say: Lo! my worship and my sacrifice and my living and my dying are for Allah, Lord of the Worlds.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Say, ‘Indeed my prayer and my worship, my life and my death are for the sake of Allah, the Lord of all the worlds.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Say: 'My prayers and my worship (for example, pilgrimage and sacrifice), my life and my death, are all for Allah, the Lord of the Worlds.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Say, "Indeed, my prayer, my rites of sacrifice, my living and my dying are for Allah, Lord of the worlds.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Say, "My prayer, sacrifice, life, and death are all for God, the Lord of the Universe.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Say. Surely my prayer and my sacrifice and my life and my death are (all) for Allah, the Lord of the worlds;

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Say, "My prayer and my sacrifice and my life and my death are all for God, the Lord of the worlds;

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Say: "Truly, my prayer and my service of sacrifice, my life and my death, are (all) for Allah, the Cherisher of the Worlds:

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qul inna salatee wanusukee wamahyaya wamamatee lillahi rabbi alAAalameena

163.arabic: لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُۥ ۖ وَبِذَٰلِكَ أُمِرْتُ وَأَنَا۠ أَوَّلُ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: No equal has He, I am commanded (to declare), and that I am the first to submit."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “He has no partner; this is what I have been commanded, and I am the first Muslim.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: No associate has He. Even so I have been commanded, and I am the first of those that surrender.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: No associate hath He. And to this I am bidden, and I am the first of the Muslims.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "He has no partner. And of this I have been commanded, and I am the first of the Muslims."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: No associate has He. Thus I am commanded, and I am the first of those who submit.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: He has no associate. Thus have I been bidden, and I am the foremost of those who submit themselves (to Allah).`

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "He has no partner. And of this I have been commanded, and I am the first of the Muslims."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He hath no partner. This am I commanded, and I am first of those who surrender (unto Him).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He has no partner, and I have been commanded [to follow] this [creed], and I am the first of those who submit [to Allah].’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: He has no partner, with that I am commanded, and I am the first of the submitters (Muslims)'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: No partner has He. And this I have been commanded, and I am the first [among you] of the Muslims."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Nothing is equal to Him. Thus are the commandments which I have received and he is the first Muslim (submitted to the will of God)."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: No associate has He; and this am I commanded, and I am the first of those who submit.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He has no partner. So am I commanded, and I am the first of those who submit."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: No partner hath He: this am I commanded, and I am the first of those who bow to His will.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: La shareeka lahu wabithalika omirtu waana awwalu almuslimeena

164.arabic: قُلْ أَغَيْرَ ٱللَّهِ أَبْغِى رَبًّا وَهُوَ رَبُّ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ ۚ وَلَا تَكْسِبُ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ إِلَّا عَلَيْهَا ۚ وَلَا تَزِرُ وَازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرَىٰ ۚ ثُمَّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكُم مَّرْجِعُكُمْ فَيُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ فِيهِ تَخْتَلِفُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Say: "Shall I search for another lord apart from God when He is the only Lord of all and everything?" Each soul earns (what it earns) for itself, and no man shall bear another's burden. You have to go back to your Lord in the end when He will tell you about the things you disputed.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Say, “Shall I seek a Lord other than Allah, whereas He is Lord of all things?” And whatever a soul earns is itself responsible for it; and no load bearing soul will bear anyone else’s load; then towards your Lord you have to return and He will inform you about the matters you differed.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Say: 'Shall I seek after a Lord other than God, who is the Lord of all things?' Every soul earns only to its own account, no soul laden bears the load of another. Then to your Lord shall you return, and He will tell you of that whereon you were at variance.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Say thou: shall seek a Lord other than Allah, while He is the Lord of everything? And no person earneth aught save against himself, and no bearer of burden shall bear anot her's burden. Thereafter Unto your Lord shall be your return, and He will declare unto you that wherein ye have been disputing.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Say: "Shall I seek a lord other than Allah, while He is the Lord of all things? No person earns any (sin) except against himself (only), and no bearer of burdens shall bear the burden of another. Then unto your Lord is your return, so He will tell you that wherein you have been differing."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Say, “Am I to seek a Lord other than God, when He is the Lord of all things?” No soul gets except what it is due, and no soul bears the burdens of another. Then to your Lord is your return, then He will inform you regarding your disputes.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Say: 'Shall I seek someone other than Allah as Lord when He is the Lord of everything?' Everyone will bear the consequence of what he does, and no one shall bear the burden of another. Thereafter, your return will be to your Lord, whereupon He will let you know what you disagreed about.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Say: "Shall I seek a lord other than Allah, while He is the Lord of all things No person earns any (sin) except against himself (only), and no bearer of burdens shall bear the burden of another. Then unto your Lord is your return, so He will tell you that wherein you have been differing."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Say: Shall I seek another than Allah for Lord, when He is Lord of all things? Each soul earneth only on its own account, nor doth any laden bear another's load. Then unto your Lord is your return and He will tell you that wherein ye differed.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Say, ‘Shall I seek a Lord other than Allah, while He is the Lord of all things?’ No soul does evil except against itself, and no bearer shall bear another’s burden; then to your Lord will be your return, whereat He will inform you concerning that about which you used to differ.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Say: 'Should I seek a lord other than Allah, who is the Lord of all things? Every soul earns only to its account no soul shall bear another's burden. Then to your Lord you shall be returned, and He will inform you about that in which you differed'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Say, "Is it other than Allah I should desire as a lord while He is the Lord of all things? And every soul earns not [blame] except against itself, and no bearer of burdens will bear the burden of another. Then to your Lord is your return, and He will inform you concerning that over which you used to differ."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad), tell them, "Should I take a lord besides God when He is the Lord of all things?" All one's evil deeds are against one's own soul. No one will be considered responsible for another's sins. You will all be returned to your Lord who will tell you what is right and wrong in disputed matters among you.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Say: What! shall I seek a Lord other than Allah? And He is the Lord of all things; and no soul earns (evil) but against itself, and no bearer of burden shall bear the burden of another; then to your Lord is your return, so He will inform you of that in which you differed.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Say, "Shall I seek a lord other than God, while He is the Lord of all things?" Everyone must bear the consequence of what he does, and no bearer of a burden can bear the burden of another. Then to your Lord you will return, and He will inform you of what you used to dispute about.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Say: "Shall I seek for (my) Cherisher other than Allah, when He is the Cherisher of all things (that exist)? Every soul draws the meed of its acts on none but itself: no bearer of burdens can bear the burden of another. Your goal in the end is towards Allah: He will tell you the truth of the things wherein ye disputed."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qul aghayra Allahi abghee rabban wahuwa rabbu kulli shayin wala taksibu kullu nafsin illa AAalayha wala taziru waziratun wizra okhra thumma ila rabbikum marjiAAukum fayunabbiokum bima kuntum feehi takhtalifoona

165.arabic: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَكُمْ خَلَـٰٓئِفَ ٱلْأَرْضِ وَرَفَعَ بَعْضَكُمْ فَوْقَ بَعْضٍ دَرَجَـٰتٍ لِّيَبْلُوَكُمْ فِى مَآ ءَاتَىٰكُمْ ۗ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ سَرِيعُ ٱلْعِقَابِ وَإِنَّهُۥ لَغَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌۢ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: It is He who made you trustees on the earth, and exalted some in rank over others in order to try you by what He has given you. Indeed your Lord's retribution is swift, yet He is forgiving and kind.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And it is He who made you caliphs (viceroys) in the earth and ranked some of you high above others, in order that He may test you with what He has bestowed upon you; indeed it does not take time for your Lord to mete out punishment; and indeed, surely, He is Oft Forgiving, Most Merciful.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: It is He who has appointed you viceroys in the earth, and has raised some of you in rank above others, that He may try you in what He has given you. Surely thy Lord is swift in retribution; and surely He is All-forgiving, All-compassionate.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And He it is Who hath made you successors in the earth, and hath raised some of you over others in degrees, that he might prove you by that which He hath vouchsafed unto you. Verily thy Lord is swift in chastising, and verily He is Forgiving, Merciful.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And it is He Who has made you generations coming after generations, replacing each other on the earth. And He has raised you in ranks, some above others that He may try you in that which He has bestowed on you. Surely your Lord is Swift in retribution, and certainly He is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: It is He who made you successors on the earth, and raised some of you in ranks over others, in order to test you through what He has given you. Your Lord is Quick in retribution, and He is Forgiving and Merciful.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: For He it is Who has appointed you vicegerent over the earth, and has exalted some of you over others in rank that He may try you in what He has bestowed is upon you. Indeed your Lord is swift in retribution, and He is certainly AllForgiving, All-Compassionate.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And it is He Who has made you generations coming after generations, replacing each other on the earth. And He has raised you in ranks, some above others that He may try you in that which He has bestowed on you. Surely, your Lord is Swift in retribution, and certainly He is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He it is Who hath placed you as viceroys of the earth and hath exalted some of you in rank above others, that He may try you by (the test of) that which He hath given you. Lo! Thy Lord is swift in prosecution, and Lo! He verily is Forgiving, Merciful.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: It is He who has made you successors on the earth, and raised some of you in rank above others so that He may test you in respect to what He has given you. Indeed your Lord is swift in retribution, and indeed, He is all-forgiving, all-merciful.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: It is He who has made you caliphs in the earth and raised some of you in rank above others, so that He might try you in what He has given you. Swift is your Lord in retribution; yet He is Forgiving, Merciful.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And it is He who has made you successors upon the earth and has raised some of you above others in degrees [of rank] that He may try you through what He has given you. Indeed, your Lord is swift in penalty; but indeed, He is Forgiving and Merciful.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: On earth, We have made each of your generations the successors of their predecessors; We have made some of you do good deeds of a higher degree than others. He will test you in this way through what He has revealed to you. Your Lord's retribution is swift and He is certainly All-forgiving and All-merciful.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And He it is Who has made you successors in the land and raised some of you above others by (various) grades, that He might try you by what He has given you; surely your Lord is quick to requite (evil), and He is most surely the Forgiving, the Merciful.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: It is He who has made you successors [of others] on the earth and has exalted some of you over the others in degrees of rank, so that He may test you by that which He has given you. Your Lord is swift in punishment; yet surely He is forgiving, and merciful.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: It is He Who hath made you (His) agents, inheritors of the earth: He hath raised you in ranks, some above others: that He may try you in the gifts He hath given you: for thy Lord is quick in punishment: yet He is indeed Oft-forgiving, Most Merciful.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wahuwa allathee jaAAalakum khalaifa alardi warafaAAa baAAdakum fawqa baAAdin darajatin liyabluwakum fee ma atakum inna rabbaka sareeAAu alAAiqabi wainnahu laghafoorun raheemun
